title: build a full-stack todo app with a Flask REST API in backend…
current_goal: how do i run this app?

current_plan.description: A simple full-stack application to manage tasks (ToDos). It uses a Flask REST API backend with SQLite and a vanilla JavaScript frontend.
current_plan.architecture: Client-Server architecture. The frontend communicates with the backend via REST API calls. The backend uses Flask for routing and SQLAlchemy for ORM/SQLite persistence.

files.purpose: Central configuration file for the application (e.g., database URI).
Defines the SQLAlchemy model for the ToDo item (e.g., id, content, completed).
Contains the API endpoints (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) for ToDo operations.
Initializes the API blueprint and registers routes.
The main Flask application entry point, setting up the database and registering blueprints.
The main HTML structure for the ToDo application, linking CSS and JS.
Basic styling for the ToDo application.
Client-side JavaScript logic to fetch, render, and manipulate ToDo items via API calls.

build_steps: Initialize database models and create tables.

subtasks.name: Setup Configuration and Database Models
subtasks.description: Establish the core settings and define the data structure for ToDos.

subtasks.name: Implement Backend API
subtasks.description: Create the Flask application structure, initialize the database, and implement the CRUD endpoints.

subtasks.name: Build Frontend Structure and Styling
subtasks.description: Create the basic HTML layout and apply minimal CSS styling.

subtasks.name: Implement Frontend Logic and Integration
subtasks.description: Write the vanilla JS logic to interact with the Flask API, handle state, and render the UI.
